nativeRange.habitat: Marine, reef-associated and non-migratory; recorded across a depth range of roughly 2-61 m (occasionally to 90 m), most often below 10 m on deeper lagoon slopes, channels and seaward reefs.

decorPreferences: mature live rock with crevices and overhangs
open swimming space across the mid-water column
subdued lighting near refuges where the fish can retreat

diet.notes: In the wild, generally considered to feed mainly on soft coral polyps (notably Sarcophyton and Litophyton spp.), algae and zooplankton. In aquaria, has been reported to accept a wide variety of meaty and prepared marine foods including mysis shrimp, finely chopped seafood, marine flake/pellet and frozen marine preparations, and has been observed eating Aiptasia pest anemones. Generally advised to feed at least 2-3 times per day, especially during acclimation.

goodWith: dwarf and large angelfishes (in suitably large systems)
avoidWith: soft corals (Sarcophyton, leathers — at risk of polyp nipping)
LPS corals and zoanthids in many cases
very aggressive triggers or large dottybacks that stress the fish
conspecifics outside of an established mated pair

summary: A peaceful Indo-Pacific butterflyfish, generally golden-yellow with a dark eye-band, that tends to be one of the hardier and easier-feeding Chaetodon species. Typically considered not fully reef-safe due to occasional nipping at soft corals, but is also valued as an Aiptasia predator.
careNotes:
Chaetodon kleinii is one of the most widely distributed butterflyfishes, ranging across the Indo-West Pacific from the Red Sea and East Africa east to Hawaii and Samoa, north to southern Japan and south to New South Wales, with an additional disjunct population in the Galapagos. It is commonly seen below 10 m on deeper lagoon slopes, channels and seaward reefs, in pairs or small groups, and is rated IUCN Least Concern.

It is commonly considered one of the easier butterflyfishes to keep: it tends to accept prepared foods more readily than most Chaetodon and is generally regarded as fairly hardy once settled. Even so, like other butterflies it benefits from a mature aquarium with stable water chemistry, plenty of live rock for refuge, and open mid-water swimming space.

Cross-source spread on minimum tank size is moderate. LiveAquaria recommends 120 US gallons (~454 L) while reefapp.net lists 300 L; FishBase confirms a maximum size around 15 cm TL. Because adults are active mid-water swimmers and are often kept in pairs, the larger end of the spread is used here, rounded up to 500 L (about 150 cm of length).

Temperature recommendations differ between sources. LiveAquaria states 72-78°F (about 22-26°C), the conservative aquarium-trade range, while FishBase wild temperature data is 24.7-29°C (mean 27.8°C). An averaged 23-27°C is used here, which spans both.

Reef-safety is mixed: the species is reported in the wild to feed mainly on soft coral polyps (notably Sarcophyton tracheliophorum and Litophyton viridis) and is widely sold as a 'not reef-safe' species, but it is also frequently kept in mixed reefs and is one of the few butterflyfishes commonly used to control Aiptasia outbreaks. It is recorded here as reef-safe with caution and best in a fish-only/FOWLR system if prize soft corals are present.

Like most butterflyfishes, it has been documented forming monogamous pairs during breeding; established pairs should not be split. Color in the trade is variable: animals can range from pale golden-yellow to more brownish, and the dark eye-band can appear black or black-and-blue, which gives rise to many of its trade names (Sunburst, Klein's, Brown, Blacklip and Corallicola Butterflyfish).
breedingNotes: Oviparous and pelagic-spawning, with monogamous mating observed during breeding. Has not been reliably bred in home aquaria; trade specimens are essentially all wild-collected.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Source of aquarium husbandry parameters (temperature range, pH, dKH, specific gravity, minimum tank size, care level, temperament, reef compatibility, diet category, feeding frequency).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Chaetodontidae, Acanthuriformes), maximum length (15.0 cm TL), Indo-West Pacific distribution, depth range (0-90 m, usually below 10 m), wild temperature range (24.7-29 °C, mean 27.8 °C), wild diet (soft coral polyps, algae and zooplankton) and reproductive mode (oviparous, monogamous, pair-bonding).

additional.name: reefapp.net
additional.notes: Additional cross-check on adult size (15 cm), peaceful temperament, hardiness/care level, and reef compatibility (described as 'reef safe with luck' — most specimens nip corals/inverts). Tank-size minimum given as 300 L; combined with LiveAquaria's 454 L, the larger value was preferred and rounded to 500 L per the playbook tank-size rule.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Used only for taxonomy and synonym cross-check (family, order, subgenus Lepidochaetodon, common-name synonyms) and to confirm Aiptasia-eating behavior in captivity.

fish.breedingNotes: Pelagic spawner that forms monogamous pairs during breeding; not known to have been reared in home aquaria.
